pattern: The combined OR/XOR move can redistribute a 1-bit anywhere in the string, so reachability only depends on whether a 1-bit exists at all, not on its position.
hints: Try the operation on a string that has at least one 1: you can shuffle that 1 around, and even create more of them, but you can never remove the last one.
A string of all zeros can never change, since combining two zero bits always produces zero bits.
So the target is reachable exactly when the source has a 1 bit if and only if the target has a 1 bit (both all-zero counts as already equal).

edge_inputs.description: both strings have ones in different positions
minimum length, both have a one
source has ones but the target is all zero
source is all zero but the target has ones
source already equals target, both with ones
a single one at opposite ends of longer strings

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    target = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    return [s, target]
